edges.evidence_quote: In May 2007, serving and former members of the PKK set up the Kurdistan Communities Union (KCK), an umbrella organisation of Kurdish organisations in Turkish, Iraqi, Iranian, and Syrian Kurdistan.
edges.context: The PKK established the KCK as an umbrella organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The PKK had an armed wing, originally formed in 1984 as the Kurdistan Freedom Brigades... renamed to the People's Liberation Army of Kurdistan... and again renamed to the People's Defence Forces (HPG) in 1999.
edges.context: The HPG is the armed wing of the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: The Free Women's Units of Star (YJA-STAR) was established in 2004 as the women's armed wing of the PKK.
edges.context: YJA-STAR is the women's armed wing of the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: The Civil Protections Units (YPS) is the successor of the Patriotic Revolutionary Youth Movement (YDG-H), the youth wing of the PKK.
edges.context: YPS is the successor of the PKK's youth wing.

edges.evidence_quote: The Civil Protections Units (YPS) is the successor of the Patriotic Revolutionary Youth Movement (YDG-H), the youth wing of the PKK.
edges.context: YDG-H was the youth wing of the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1985, the National Liberation Front of Kurdistan (ERNK) was established by the PKK as its popular front wing.
edges.context: ERNK was the popular front wing of the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: The PKK has in March 2016 also vowed to overthrow the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, through the 'Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement'.
edges.context: The PKK was part of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.

edges.context: Tevgera Azadî is said to be close to the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: Former police special forces member Ayhan Çarkın said that the state, using the clandestine Ergenekon network, colluded with militant groups such as the PKK, Dev-Sol and Turkish Hezbollah, with the goal of profiting from the war.
edges.context: Ergenekon colluded with the PKK.

edges.evidence_quote: Former police special forces member Ayhan Çarkın said that the state, using the clandestine Ergenekon network, colluded with militant groups such as the PKK, Dev-Sol and Turkish Hezbollah, with the goal of profiting from the war.
edges.context: PKK and Dev-Sol are both mentioned as groups that colluded with Ergenekon, but no direct relationship between them is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: Former police special forces member Ayhan Çarkın said that the state, using the clandestine Ergenekon network, colluded with militant groups such as the PKK, Dev-Sol and Turkish Hezbollah, with the goal of profiting from the war.
edges.context: PKK and Turkish Hezbollah are both mentioned as groups that colluded with Ergenekon, but no direct relationship between them is stated.

edges.target: Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine
edges.evidence_quote: The PKK also managed to come to agreements with the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine (DFLP), the Fatah of Yassir Arafat or the Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) of Samir Ghawshah and also with Masoud Barzani's Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP) in Iraqi Kurdistan which would facilitate their activities.
edges.context: PKK came to agreements with DFLP to facilitate activities.

edges.evidence_quote: The PKK also managed to come to agreements with the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine (DFLP), the Fatah of Yassir Arafat or the Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) of Samir Ghawshah and also with Masoud Barzani's Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP) in Iraqi Kurdistan which would facilitate their activities.
edges.context: PKK came to agreements with Fatah to facilitate activities.

edges.evidence_quote: The PKK also managed to come to agreements with the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine (DFLP), the Fatah of Yassir Arafat or the Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) of Samir Ghawshah and also with Masoud Barzani's Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP) in Iraqi Kurdistan which would facilitate their activities.
edges.context: PKK came to agreements with PPSF to facilitate activities.

edges.evidence_quote: The PKK also managed to come to agreements with the Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine (DFLP), the Fatah of Yassir Arafat or the Palestinian Popular Struggle Front (PPSF) of Samir Ghawshah and also with Masoud Barzani's Kurdistan Democratic Party (KDP) in Iraqi Kurdistan which would facilitate their activities.
edges.context: PKK came to agreements with KDP to facilitate activities.

edges.evidence_quote: The TAK is a rival to PKK that since 2006 repeatedly damaged the PKK's efforts to negotiate cease-fires and unlike the PKK, is seeking to establish independent Kurdistan.
edges.context: TAK is a rival to PKK, damaging its ceasefire efforts.

edges.target: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
edges.evidence_quote: The PKK engaged the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant (ISIL) forces in Syria in mid-July 2014 as part of the Syrian Civil War.
edges.context: The PKK fought against ISIL in Syria and Iraq.

edges.evidence_quote: The Turkish government continued to restrict PKK-associated fighters' movement across its borders, arresting 260 People's Defense Units fighters who were moving back into Turkey.
edges.context: The PKK is associated with the People's Defense Units (YPG), as YPG fighters are considered PKK-associated.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP , TKEP/L , TİKB , DKP , DK and MLKP ) with the aim of overthrowing the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.
edges.context: The PKK co-founded the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other groups.

edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP , TKEP/L , TİKB , DKP , DK and MLKP ) with the aim of overthrowing the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.
edges.context: The PKK cooperated with TKP/ML as part of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.

edges.target: Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia
edges.evidence_quote: Between the PKK and the Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia (ASALA) a cooperation has been agreed on in April 1980 in Sidon, Lebanon.
edges.context: The PKK and ASALA agreed on cooperation in April 1980.
